steps.title: Use the exponential growth form
steps.content:
For yearly exponential growth,
$$p(t)=a(1+r)^t$$
where $a$ is the starting amount and $r$ is the annual growth rate as a decimal.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content:
The starting population is $30$, so $a=30$. A $14.8\%$ increase means $r=0.148$, so the growth factor is $1.148$.
$$p(t)=30(1.148)^t$$

steps.title: Compare to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice C (‘$p(t) = 30 \times 1.148^t$’) has the correct starting population as the coefficient and the correct annual growth factor as the base. C is correct.
